reasoning: Francesca Muñoz is a minor political figure in Chile, serving as a deputy and representing the Partido Social Cristiano (PSC), a relatively small right-wing conservative evangelical party. The main contenders for the right in the 2025 election are widely established to be Evelyn Matthei (Chile Vamos) and José Antonio Kast (Republicans), both of whom heavily dominate polling and media coverage. As a minor faction candidate, Muñoz lacks the widespread national support, financial backing, and party machinery required to realistically contend for the presidency in a polarized and consolidated political landscape. The base rate for obscure third-party or single-issue candidates winning the Chilean presidency is practically zero. Therefore, her chances of winning the 2025 election are negligible, leading to an estimate at the lowest allowed bound.
